concepts.title: account holder
concepts.definition: party that owns an account

concepts.title: agent
concepts.definition: something autonomous that can adapt to and interact with its environment

concepts.title: client
concepts.definition: party that purchases professional services from, or has a formal relationship to purchase services from another party

concepts.title: contract party
concepts.definition: legally competent party that has entered into a binding agreement, accepting and conceding obligations, responsibilities, and benefits as specified

concepts.title: customer
concepts.definition: party that receives or consumes products (goods or services) and has the ability to choose between different products and suppliers

concepts.title: customer account
concepts.definition: account that represents an identified, named collection of balances and cumulative totals used to summarize customer transaction-related activity over a designated period of time

concepts.title: customer account holder
concepts.definition: party that owns a customer account

concepts.title: legally competent natural person
concepts.definition: person who is considered competent, under the circumstances, to enter into a contract, conduct business, or participate in other activities that generally require the mental ability to understand problems and make decisions on his or her own behalf

concepts.title: party
concepts.definition: person or organization

concepts.title: person
concepts.definition: individual human being, with consciousness of self

concepts.title: person name
concepts.definition: designation by which someone is known in some context

concepts.title: business identifier code
concepts.definition: international identifier for financial and non-financial institutions used to facilitate automated processing of information for financial services

concepts.title: client identifier
concepts.definition: sequence of characters uniquely identifying a client within the context of some organization

concepts.title: customer identifier
concepts.definition: sequence of characters uniquely identifying a customer within the context of some organization

concepts.title: driver's license identifier
concepts.definition: identifier associated with a drivers' or operating license for operating a motor vehicle or non-driver identification card

concepts.title: entity legal form identifier
concepts.definition: code that denotes an entity legal form as defined in ISO 20275

concepts.title: identity document
concepts.definition: any legal document which may be used to verify aspects of a person's identity

concepts.title: legal entity identifier
concepts.definition: an organization identifier that uniquely identifies a legal person as defined in ISO 17442

concepts.title: legal entity identifier scheme
concepts.definition: a scheme that specifies the elements of an unambiguous legal entity identifier (LEI) scheme to identify the legal entities relevant to any financial transaction

concepts.title: LEI registered entity
concepts.definition: a legal person that has registered for and is identified by a legal entity identifier

concepts.title: chartered legal person
concepts.definition: a legal person created by a royal charter or decree

concepts.title: corporation
concepts.definition: formal organization that is a legal entity (artificial person) distinct from its owners, created under the jurisdiction of the laws of a state or nation

concepts.title: for profit corporation
concepts.definition: corporation whose objective is to make money, i.e., to ensure realization of a financial benefit such that the amount of revenue gained from a business activity exceeds the expenses, costs and taxes needed to sustain that activity

concepts.title: formal organization
concepts.definition: organization that is recognized in some legal jurisdiction, with associated rights and responsibilities

relations.target_title: North American Industry Classification System code

concepts.title: legal entity
concepts.definition: legal person that is a partnership, corporation, or other organization having the capacity to negotiate contracts, assume financial obligations, and pay off debts, organized under the laws of some jurisdiction

relations.target_title: North American Industry Classification System code

concepts.title: legal person
concepts.definition: party that is recognized as having rights and obligations under the law, including but not limited to the right to sue and be sued, enter into contracts, own property, and incur financial and other obligations

concepts.title: non-governmental organization
concepts.definition: not-for-profit organization that functions independently of government

concepts.title: not for profit organization
concepts.definition: organization that uses its surplus revenues to further achieve its purpose rather than distributing its surplus income to the organization's owners (directors, investors, and equivalents) as profit / dividends

concepts.title: not-for-profit corporation
concepts.definition: corporation approved by its jurisdictional oversight and tax authorities as operating for educational, charitable, social, religious, civic or humanitarian purposes

concepts.title: organization
concepts.definition: framework of authority within which a person, persons, or groups of people act, or are designated to act, towards some purpose, such as to meet a need or pursue collective goals

concepts.title: beneficial owner
concepts.definition: party that enjoys the benefits of ownership (such as receipt of income) of something even though its ownership (title) may be in the name of another party (called a nominee or registered owner)

concepts.title: beneficial ownership
concepts.definition: situation linking party that enjoys the benefits of ownership (such as receipt of income) of something even though its ownership (title) may be in the name of another party (called a nominee or registered owner) to the asset that they own

concepts.title: board composition control
concepts.definition: situation in which a voting shareholder, entity owner, or some other party in the case of a not-for-profit organization, appoints and/or nominates someone to the board of directors of an organization for some period of time

concepts.title: contractual control
concepts.definition: control established through the terms of some contract

concepts.title: control
concepts.definition: situation in which some party has the power to direct or strongly influence the direction of the management and policies related to something

concepts.title: controlled party
concepts.definition: person or organization over which some party exercises some form of authority or influence with respect to some situation

concepts.title: controlling equity
concepts.definition: shareholders's equity that formally confers control in the entity, either by law or as explicitly stated in a corresponding equity instrument

concepts.title: controlling party
concepts.definition: actor that exercises some form of control in the context of some situation

concepts.title: de facto control
concepts.definition: control that exists informally and is accepted, although not formally recognized

concepts.title: de facto controlling interest party
concepts.definition: party that exercises some control over an entity other than via explicit, legal means

concepts.title: de jure control
concepts.definition: control that exists as a matter of law, i.e., legitimate, legal control of something

concepts.title: de jure controlling interest party
concepts.definition: party that has the legal authority to exercise control

concepts.title: entity controlling party
concepts.definition: party that has the authority to control some legal entity

concepts.title: entity ownership
concepts.definition: ownership by some party of an interest in some non-governmental formal organization

concepts.title: majority controlling party
concepts.definition: controlling party that possesses, either directly or indirectly, the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a legal person, whether through the ownership of a majority of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise

concepts.title: ownership
concepts.definition: situation in which some party holds the legal title to something (explicitly or implicitly) and has the right to transfer that title and/or possession

concepts.title: ownership control situation
concepts.definition: situation in which some party owns and controls something

concepts.title: general partnership
concepts.definition: partnership that has at least two general partners that agree to share in all assets, profits, and financial and legal liabilities of the business

concepts.title: irrevocable trust
concepts.definition: trust that cannot be modified, amended or terminated except under certain legal circumstances and typically not without the permission of the grantor's named beneficiary or beneficiaries

concepts.title: limited liability partnership
concepts.definition: partnership that has general partners but provides its individual partners some level of protection against personal liability for certain partnership liabilities

concepts.title: limited partnership
concepts.definition: partnership that has at least one general partner and at least one limited partner

concepts.title: living trust
concepts.definition: trust created during an individual's lifetime where a designated person, the trustee, is given responsibility for managing that individual's assets for the benefit of the eventual beneficiary

concepts.title: partnership
concepts.definition: association of two or more legal persons to carry on as co-owners a business for profit

concepts.title: trust
concepts.definition: fiduciary relationship and legal entity in which one party, known as a trustor, gives another party, the trustee, the right to hold title to and manage assets for the benefit of a third party, the beneficiary

concepts.title: business register identifier
concepts.definition: identifier that uniquely identifies a business register, such as a register identified by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF) registration authorities list

concepts.title: business registration authority
concepts.definition: registration authority that is responsible for maintaining a registry of business entities

concepts.title: legal entity identifier registry
concepts.definition: registry for registering and maintaining information about business entities for a particular jurisdiction

concepts.title: registration authority code
concepts.definition: identifier that uniquely identifies a business registry, and is associated with a registration authority and jurisdiction, issued by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF)

bridges.rationale: For KYC and entity resolution a legal entity is identified by its Legal Entity Identifier (ISO 17442). FIBO defines LegalEntityIdentifier and applies it to LEI-registered entities, but does not link the base LegalEntity class to it, leaving generic entity-to-LEI resolution implicit.

A beneficial owner is the party that ultimately owns or controls a legal entity. FIBO models BeneficialOwnership as a situation but draws no direct edge from the BeneficialOwner role to the legal entity owned, which KYC / ultimate-beneficial-owner resolution needs.
bridges.citation: FATF Recommendations 24 & 25; US CDD rule 31 CFR 1010.230

bridges.rationale: A customer is a party role played by a legal person (or a natural person). Resolving 'the customer' to the underlying legal entity is core to KYC onboarding, but FIBO leaves the role-to-entity link implicit.
bridges.citation: FIBO FND party-role vs entity modeling; KYC customer onboarding

bridges.rationale: An institutional account holder is identified for KYC by its Legal Entity Identifier. FIBO models accounts and their holders but does not connect an account holder to its LEI.
